name: Big Maybelle
abstract: Mabel Louise Smith (May 1, 1924 – January 23, 1972), known professionally as Big Maybelle, was an American R&B singer. Her 1956 hit single "Candy" received the Grammy Hall of Fame Award in 1999.

concerts.label: Blues at Carnegie: Muddy Waters, Jimmy Witherspoon, Jimmy Reed, Oscar Brown, Jr., Big Maybelle

about_quotes.speaker: Scott Jimmy
about_quotes.evidence: She was like a little sister to me. ... But she was your close personal friend, and you loved hanging out with her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Scott Jimmy — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham
about_quotes.evidence: Big Maybelle and Jimmy Witherspoon and Dinah Washington... And I had never played
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy and Jeannie Cheatham interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 12, 1998
